reasoning: A better enables a correct answer because it contains Debian-specific evidence: a Debian mailing-list bug report stating that ten official Debian base images on Docker Hub still contained the xz backdoor in August 2025, plus the corresponding GitHub issue requesting removal and showing it was closed, and a reputable media report summarizing Binarly's finding that affected images were still on Docker Hub. Those facts are directly relevant to both the findings and status. B mostly contains generic CVE background and an uninformative Binarly snippet, so an LLM would not have enough grounded information to answer the Debian Docker image findings/removal-status question correctly.
